无.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a person dancing with ox tails in both hands to pray for rain; later borrowed for the meaning 'have no', with 舞 adopted for the original sense.
无.difference: Our data classifies 無 as phono_semantic_compound, but Wiktionary identifies it as a pictogram depicting a rain-prayer dance.

力.wiki_summary: Pictogram of either an arm bending out from the side of the body, or a wooden plough.

它.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a snake (now written as 蛇); later borrowed via jiajie for the third-person pronoun.
它.difference: Our data classifies 它 as phono_semantic_compound (from 宀 + 也), but Wiktionary identifies it as a pictogram of a snake later borrowed for pronoun use.

与.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with two phonetic components: 舁 (OC *la) and 牙 (OC *ŋraː), both serving as phonetics.
与.difference: Our data classifies 與 as compound_ideograph (semantic 勺+舁), but Wiktionary identifies it as a phono-semantic compound with dual phonetic components 舁 and 牙.

长.wiki_summary: Pictogram of an old man with long hair; an ancient variant 镸 preserves this form.

把.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 扌 (hand) + phonetic 巴 (OC *praː).

机.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 木 (wood) + phonetic 幾 (OC *kɯl).

民.wiki_summary: Pictogram: oracle bone script forms depict an eye pierced by a cross-shaped sharp implement.
民.difference: Our data classifies 民 as simple_indicative, but Wiktionary clearly identifies it as a pictogram based on oracle bone script evidence.

第.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 竹 (bamboo) + phonetic 弟 (OC *diːls).

公.wiki_summary: Traditionally analysed as compound of 八 (deviate/opposite) and 厶 (private), meaning the opposite of private — public; alternative theories include a pictogram of a jar and a distribution metaphor.
